title: Jesus, the very thought of Thee
firstLine: Jesus, the very thought of Thee
textAttribution: Text: Bernard of Clairvaux (1090–1153), Edward Caswall (1814–1878).
publicDomainBasis: Bernard of Clairvaux (1090–1153), Edward Caswall (1814–1878), John Bacchus Dykes (1823–1876). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Jesus, the very thought of Thee
With sweetness fills my breast;
But sweeter far Thy face to see,
And in Thy presence rest.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Nor voice can sing, nor heart can frame,
Nor can the memory find
A sweeter sound than Thy blest Name,
O Savior of mankind!

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
O Hope of every contrite heart,
O Joy of all the meek,
To those who fall, how kind Thou art!
How good to those who seek!

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
But what to those who find? Ah, this
Nor tongue nor pen can show;
The love of Jesus, what it is
None but His loved ones know.

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
O Jesus! light of all below!
Thou fount of life and fire!
Surpassing all the joys we know,
And all we can desire.

sections.label: Verse 6
sections.text:
No other source have we but Thee,
Soul-thirst to satisfy.
Exhaustless spring! the waters free!
All other streams are dry.

sections.label: Verse 7
sections.text:
Jesus, our only Joy be Thou,
As Thou our Prize wilt be;
Jesus, be Thou our Glory now,
And through eternity.
